macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Surges Past $78,000 on Iran Ceasefire Extension and Institutional Demand
items.summary: Bitcoin jumped 3.7% to $78,712, reaching a high of $78,780, driven by U.S. President Trump's extension of the Iran ceasefire and strong institutional buying. MicroStrategy acquired 34,164 BTC for $2.5 billion, boosting its holdings to 815,000 coins.

items.title: Bitcoin Breaks $79,000 Amid Short Squeeze Liquidating $286 Million
items.summary: Bitcoin broke above $79,000 and traded at $78,800, surpassing the $78,000 resistance and triggering a $286 million short squeeze. This marks an 11-week high fueled by risk-on sentiment.

items.title: Altcoins Rise with Bitcoin Rally; Ethereum Up 3.2% to $2,391
items.summary: Ethereum gained 3.2% to $2,391, XRP rose 1.3% to $1.46, while Solana, Cardano, Polygon each climbed 2.5% and Dogecoin increased 2.3%. Gains were more limited compared to Bitcoin's surge.

items.title: Arbitrum Security Council Freezes $71 Million in ETH After Kelp Exploit
items.summary: Arbitrum froze 30,766 ETH worth $71 million linked to the Kelp exploit, recovering about a quarter of stolen funds via a governance-controlled wallet. This follows similar actions in wet markets.

items.title: Circle Faces Class Action Lawsuit Over Failure to Freeze Hacked Stablecoins
items.summary: Circle is sued for not intervening in a hack by freezing tokens without a court order, highlighting stablecoin risks amid rising market volatility.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Georgia Tech Researchers Unveil Diffusion-DFL: New AI Model for Decision-Making Under Uncertainty
items.summary: Georgia Tech has created Diffusion-DFL, a decision-focused learning model based on diffusion technology that makes more accurate decisions in real-world scenarios like factory manufacturing and power grid scheduling. The model reduces training costs by over 99.7% while maintaining high performance, making it accessible to smaller enterprises.

items.title: MIT Researchers Develop RLCR Method to Improve AI Confidence Calibration
items.summary: MIT's CSAIL lab has created a training technique called RLCR that teaches AI models to express appropriate uncertainty alongside their answers, reducing calibration error by up to 90% without sacrificing accuracy. The method addresses a root cause of AI hallucinations and will be presented at ICLR 2026.

items.title: Anthropic Releases Claude Opus 4.7 with Enhanced Capabilities
items.summary: Anthropic has released Claude Opus 4.7, featuring 87.6% performance on SWE-bench Verified, 94.2% on GPQA benchmarks, 1M token context window, and 3.3x higher-resolution vision capabilities. Pricing is set at $5/$25.

items.title: Google Launches Partner-Built Agents in Gemini Enterprise
items.summary: Google has integrated partner-built AI agents from companies including Accenture, Adobe, Atlassian, Deloitte, Salesforce, and ServiceNow directly into the Gemini Enterprise app's Agent Gallery. The company also announced the Google for Startups AI Agents Challenge.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Call of Duty Modern Warfare 4 Not Launching Day One on Xbox and PC Game Pass
items.summary: Leaked information reveals Modern Warfare 4 will not release day one on Xbox and PC Game Pass, potentially impacting its launch success. The decision is seen as both positive and negative for the franchise.

items.title: Crimson Desert Map Expansion Confirmed as Reality
items.summary: Pearl Abyss has developed extensive lands beyond the current borders of Crimson Desert, strongly indicating future map expansions. An expansion pack is considered 100% likely.

items.title: Intel Core Ultra 7 270k Plus Released, Dominates Competition
items.summary: Intel's new Core Ultra 7 270k Plus processor outperforms all competitors, offering top performance for video editing and 3D rendering. It is hailed as the best news of 2026 for creative professionals.

items.title: Replaced Game Preview Shows Outstanding Progress, Among 2026's Most Anticipated
items.summary: A preview of Replaced highlights it as one of the most anticipated games of 2026, with outstanding quality. Servers remain active amid a wave of game releases.

items.title: Marvel Contest of Champions Announces Bug Compensations and Updates
items.summary: Upcoming compensations address AQ leaderboard bugs, black screen issues, and more in Marvel Contest of Champions. New events include Deadpool's Spring Cleaning 2026 and Act 9.4 rewards.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: SK Hynix Plans $12.85B Investment in New South Korea Fab for Advanced Packaging
items.summary: SK Hynix announced plans to invest approximately $12.85 billion in a new fabrication facility in South Korea focused on advanced packaging for high-bandwidth memory (HBM) to meet demand, with construction starting this month.

items.title: France Ditches Windows for Linux to Reduce Reliance on American Tools
items.summary: France is transitioning from Windows to Linux, mirroring a similar shift in India, as part of efforts to move away from American software dependencies.

items.title: HCLTech Reports Q4 Net Profit Up 4.2% YoY to Rs 4,488 Crore
items.summary: HCLTech's Q4 net profit rose 4.2% year-over-year to Rs 4,488 crore, with revenue increasing 12%, and the company declared a Rs 24 per share dividend.

items.title: Infosys to Discuss CEO Salil Parekh's Tenure and Succession Roadmap
items.summary: Infosys is set to discuss CEO Salil Parekh's tenure extension and a succession plan during its Q4 results announcement.

items.title: US Senator Elizabeth Warren Raises Concerns Over Nvidia's Acquisition of Slurm Developer
items.summary: Senator Warren expressed concerns about Nvidia's acquisition of SchedMD, the developer of Slurm, citing potential national security risks and control over software used in US government supercomputers.

items.title: Australia's Syenta Raises $26 Million to Address AI Chip Bottleneck
items.summary: Syenta, an Australian startup, secured $26 million in funding to ease AI chip supply constraints, with former Intel CEO joining its board.
